result.response_summary: Unable to submit prompt to Gemini. Text was successfully entered into the Quill editor input field and Angular detected it (send button became active/green), but NO method could programmatically trigger the send button click.
methods_attempted: 1. UI Automation Click by label (Snapshot) - tooltip '제출' appears but no submit
3. JS querySelector('[aria-label="메시지 보내기"]').click() - returns undefined, no submit
4. JS PointerEvent + MouseEvent dispatch chain - returns true, no submit
5. P/Invoke SetCursorPos + mouse_event - DPI 125% coordinate mismatch
6. Tab + Enter on focused button - no effect
7. Tab + Space on focused button - no effect
8. Type tool press_enter in input - creates newline (Quill editor)
9. Ctrl+V paste then Enter in input - creates newline
11. JS execCommand('insertText') + setTimeout click - CSP errors, no submit
12. DevTools Console: PointerEvent pointerdown/pointerup + MouseEvent click dispatch - returns true, no submit
13. DevTools Console: btn.focus() + btn.click() - no submit
14. Click tool label-based click (fresh Snapshot) - tooltip appears, no submit
15. Multiple retry clicks at (1437,596) - hover only, no submit
result.root_cause: Gemini uses Angular Material + zone.js framework. Zone.js patches native event listeners and only responds to genuine browser-initiated user input events. All programmatic methods (UI Automation InvokePattern, JS dispatchEvent, P/Invoke mouse_event) generate synthetic events that zone.js does not process through Angular's change detection pipeline. The send button receives hover/tooltip but Angular's click handler never fires.
result.console_errors: Multiple CSP violations (connect-src, img-src for Google ads/tracking). Fetch API blocks for googleadservices.com. These are pre-existing and unrelated to submission failure.
result.note: Phase3-B6: Gemini is_http2=1 + GOAWAY=true test COULD NOT BE EXECUTED due to automation limitation. Gemini's Angular Material framework actively prevents programmatic button activation. Recommend manual testing or Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) remote debugging approach for future attempts.
